question: In Gethsemane, how does ‘not my will but yours’ function as a window into (a) real human will and (b) perfect obedience—without collapsing into ‘two gods’ confusion?

choices.A: It shows Jesus’ genuine human will aligning with the Father’s will in obedient love, which supports full humanity without dividing God into competing deities.
choices.B: It proves Jesus is not divine and is unrelated to God.
choices.C: It proves there are two gods with different plans.
choices.D: It shows Jesus is pretending to be human.

teaching.title: Two wills, one obedient mission
teaching.body: The prayer highlights real human desire and real submission—obedience, not theatricality. Apologetically, it supports classical Christian claims that Jesus is fully human (with a human will) while remaining united with the Father’s divine will, avoiding both ‘not really human’ and ‘two gods’ misreads.
